Title: THE ORIGINS OF MODERN MILITARISM: LESSONS FROM HISTORY
Small Description: An introduction to the history of the formation of the ideology and practices of modern militarism.

Title: MILITARISM IN THE 20TH AND 21ST CENTURIES: FORGOTTEN LESSONS OF MODERN HISTORY
Small Description: How militarism has caught hundreds of millions of people in its web and how it serves to maintain modern political and economic systems.

Title: Militaristic Ideologies and Traditions in the Russian Empire and the Soviet Union
MILITARISM IN THE PERIOD OF LIBERAL PEACEBUILDING
Small Description: How extensive was the "demilitarization" after the end of the Cold War and was there any hope for an “end of history” in terms of wars and militarism?

Title: WHAT IS MILITARISM? SOME THEORY
Small Description: The academic tradition of critical studies of militarism. We will learn about the principle researchers and their views.

Title: MILITARISM AND THE PROBLEM OF VIOLENCE
Small Description: The relationship between militarism and violence is more complex than it seems at first glance. How various forms of violence manifest in everyday life in militaristic symbols and practices.

Title: MILITARISM IN THE LANGUAGE OF EVERYDAY LIFE
Small Description: The pervasiveness of militarism and how we discursively reproduce it without even realizing it.

Title: WHAT HISTORY ARE WE TAUGHT? MILITARISM IN CONCEPTIONS OF THE PAST
Small Description: How our national histories are written in such a way as to justify wars and glorify militaristic violence.

Title: MILITARISM LANDSCAPES
Small Description: How militarism is reflected in historical architectural heritage, sites of remembrance, and memorial landscapes.

Title: MILITARISM AND THE MAP
Small Description: How maps are often a tool of war, reinforcing nationalistic ideas and visualizing myths about "historical territories".
